other_observers: Elena Thomas Faulkner; Bill Schmoker, m.ob.
description: A small, dark goose about the size of a Lesser Canada Goose (B. canadensis parvipes).  The remiges, rectrices, head, neck, and extension from the neck onto the belly were very dark brown-black. The vent region was pure white as were the uppertail coverts. The remainder of the body was a dark brown, with lighter brown on the flanks. Greater coverts were edged in pale brown, the same color as the flanks. On both sides of the neck were obscure white v-shaped patches that connected in front. All soft parts were black.Behaviors: Swimming with other waterfowl in pond.Call: None heard.Plumage: 1st basic
